page: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the Ddsv7 family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. The configuration includes 64 virtual CPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It features a large L3 cache of 480 MB, 256.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core, and 3780 GB of local storage. The instance runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark data shows strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 67260.4 and a PassMark CPU score of 86642.75, placing it in the top tier. While single-core performance is average with a stress-ng score of 2256.48, multi-threaded capabilities are robust, as demonstrated by a bzip3 multi-threaded decompression rate of 432.41 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is also strong, reaching 326.02 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 127.56 GB/sec for large block write operations.

The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions, which accelerate vector and matrix operations. In database and web serving benchmarks, the instance achieves top-tier results, with 32363.78 operations per second in PassMark database tests and a static web serving throughput of 104.9 GB/sec. Redis SET operations reach 111.2 million operations per second. These characteristics make the instance suitable for relational databases, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded data processing workloads that benefit from local storage and high memory bandwidth.
description: The Microsoft Azure D64ds_v7 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine from the Ddsv7 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. It features 64 vCPUs, 256.0 GB of RAM, and 3780 GB of local storage, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with a PassMark CPU score of 86642.75 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 67260.4. The instance excels in data-intensive tasks, achieving top-tier performance in static web serving and database operations. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX for accelerated vector and matrix computations. This hardware profile is optimized for database hosting, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded enterprise workloads requiring high local storage capacity and balanced memory-to-CPU ratios.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 3780 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier database performance and high-throughput static web serving capabilities.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, optimized for database and web workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring sixty-four virtual CPUs, substantial local storage, and advanced matrix acceleration for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 256 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor
3780 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier database performance and 104.9 GB/sec static web serving throughput
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated vector math
Strong multi-core processing and high-bandwidth memory architecture
